entry.title: SAP PP & Manufacturing Certification Still not passed
entry.content:
Hello everybody 

I had used my attempt from my University to take the SAP PP & Manufacturing certification (C_TS422) with the new format exam System based, i had take it twice and still not passed. 

I do checked it like 6 times reviewing it, studying again from SAP Learning hub and i used the SAP GUI system, and i do re check using Claude and Gemini, but the result still below 50. Anyone have the same issue? or anyone have tips and trick for my future certification exam

Do you guys know how the SAP examination our system based exam?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: HU Screen Not Visible During Goods Receipt with Produ...
entry.content:
Good day @kkumara 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your screenshot and description, you are trying to receive goods for production order into an EWM manage storage location.

The first thing to clarify is that simply extending the material to the warehouse storage location is not enough to make the HU screen appear. For production order goods receipt, the HU and Warehouse Management fields depend on the activated warehouse production integration process, the receiving storage location, synchronous goods movement settings and whether the production GR is processed with or without an EWM inbound delivery.

Note that goods receipt can be posted manually, for example with Post Goods Movement app, automatically with Confirm Production Order/Confirm Production Order Operation. The receiving storage location is taken from the production order header.

For EWM integration, Warehouse Production Integration with Synchronous Goods Receipt 5HO allow produced products to be posted directly into the warehouse without delivery documents and the process can include Pack Finished Material, Create HU Putaway Warehouse Task, and Confirm Putaway Warehouse Tasks.

So, in simple terms:

  • If the production GR is not relevant for the warehouse integration scenario, the HU or Warehouse Management section will not appear.
  • IF the storage location in the production order is not the EWM managed receiving storage location, the warehouse tab will not behave as expected.
  • If synchronous goods movement is not active or not correctly mapped for the storage location, the Warehouse Management tab my not be shown.
  • Material extension alone does not activate the HU screen.
  • Depending on your design, the process must be aligned with scope item 3DV Warehouse Production Integration or 5HO Warehouse Production Integration with Synchronous Goods Receipt

See KBA 3249204 which is very close to your situation, and it explains the system logic for determining the storage bin when posting goods receipt for production orders with or without inbound deliveries to an EWM warehouse. Also check KBA 3451929 for confirmation on HU handling unit being relevant for goods receipt without delivery in the Post Goods Movement app.

Basically, this means the following:

  • There is no separate material master field that simply “turns on” the HU screen for production GR.
  • First check whether your production order GR is being posted into an EWM managed storage location.
  • Then check whether the process is delivery based or synchronous goods movement based.
  • If it is delivery based, you normally check the warehouse information through the production inbound delivery process.
  • If it’s synchronous goods receipt, the Warehouse Management tab and HU related actions depend on the synchronous goods movement setup and warehouse mapping.

See KBA 3498212 for more details tab visibility and its dependencies on exact business process and movement scenario.

 

To summarise:

The HU screen is not controlled only by the fact that you extended the material to a warehouse storage location. For production order goods receipt, the system must treat the receipt as warehouse relevant. This depends on the production order receiving storage location, the activated warehouse production integration scope, the storage location to warehouse mapping, and whether you use synchronous goods movement or inbound delivery-based process.

As a next step, check the receiving storage location, confirm that it is mapped to the warehouse and confirm the warehouse production integration scope is active. Then test the same order in Post Goods Movement with movement type 101 

If the process creates or uses inbound delivery, check it in Change Inbound Deliveries-Production.   

If the setup is correct and the tab is still missing only for production order GR, then raise a case with SAP.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: How to find authorization roles with an object and specific field values tied together?
entry.content:
Hello, 

I need some help. 

Im trying to export a list of users with specific authorization roles that have a specific authorization object as well as specific field values. 

The object ist called /MDS/ORGAN  - and under this object I need to find every role (or user but with all the roles I can find the users) that has the field /MDS/OBJTY with the value MATERIAL as well as the field ACTVT with the value 02. 

I tried the SE16 Table AGR_1251 where I can search for multiple fields in an object but its just an "either or" query. I need to have both tied together. 

I hope this makes sense and somebody can help me. 

Thank you very much in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA PP Certification – What is the new system...
entry.content: Hello Jan thank you for your insight, i had review my system based exam 6x times, with twice attempt that i have used but still not passed are you alredy passed and can share some tips, i user GUI to run the configuration, master data, and end to end process thank you

entry.title: Batch number not generated for multipe co products on releasing process order
entry.content:
Faced issue on batch generation for multiple coproduct on releasing the process order. Later got the issue.

I have created new material with out coproduct check and added to BOM as component. Later released it. System not generated batch for co product.
Later added coproduct at material master and created new process order and released. Still not got the batch.

Then i got to know that the co product check is not updated in BOM as i created it with change number. Then I have added the co product check in BOM manually and then created new process order and released. Now its generating the Batch for co product. 


So the system checking the coproduct check at BOM level for generating the batch.

Regards:

Amaresh

entry.title: Re: Need Suggestions for FB60 Mass Upload (Non-PO) – BDC vs BAPI
entry.content:
Check this code and modify your code accordingly 

Code :

 
TYPES : BEGIN OF LTY_DATA,
        MATNR TYPE MATNR,
        MBRSH TYPE MBRSH,
        MTART TYPE MTART,
        MAKTX TYPE MAKTX,
        MEINS TYPE MEINS,
       END OF LTY_DATA.
 
DATA : LT_DATA TYPE TABLE OF LTY_DATA,
       LS_DATA TYPE LTY_DATA.
 
DATA : LV_FILE TYPE STRING.
 
DATA : LT_BDCDATA TYPE TABLE OF BDCDATA,
       LS_BDCDATA TYPE BDCDATA.
 
data : lt_MESSTAB type table of BDCMSGCOLL,
       ls_messtab type BDCMSGCOLL.
 
DATA : LV_MESSAGE TYPE STRING.
 
 
PARAMETERS : P_FILE TYPE LOCALFILE.
 
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR P_FILE.
 
CALL FUNCTION 'F4_FILENAME'
  EXPORTING
    PROGRAM_NAME = SYST-CPROG
    DYNPRO_NUMBER = SYST-DYNNR
    FIELD_NAME = ' '
  IMPORTING
    FILE_NAME = P_FILE.
 
          .
 
START-OF-SELECTION.
 
LV_FILE = P_FILE.
 
CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_UPLOAD'
  EXPORTING
    FILENAME = lv_file
* FILETYPE = 'ASC'
    HAS_FIELD_SEPARATOR = 'X'
* HEADER_LENGTH = 0
* READ_BY_LINE = 'X'
* DAT_MODE = ' '
* CODEPAGE = ' '
* IGNORE_CERR = ABAP_TRUE
* REPLACEMENT = '#'
* CHECK_BOM = ' '
* VIRUS_SCAN_PROFILE =
* NO_AUTH_CHECK = ' '
* IMPORTING
* FILELENGTH =
* HEADER =
  TABLES
    DATA_TAB = LT_DATA
* CHANGING
* ISSCANPERFORMED = ' '
  EXCEPTIONS
    FILE_OPEN_ERROR = 1
    FILE_READ_ERROR = 2
    NO_BATCH = 3
    GUI_REFUSE_FILETRANSFER = 4
    INVALID_TYPE = 5
    NO_AUTHORITY = 6
    UNKNOWN_ERROR = 7
    BAD_DATA_FORMAT = 8
    HEADER_NOT_ALLOWED = 9
    SEPARATOR_NOT_ALLOWED = 10
    HEADER_TOO_LONG = 11
    UNKNOWN_DP_ERROR = 12
    ACCESS_DENIED = 13
    DP_OUT_OF_MEMORY = 14
    DISK_FULL = 15
    DP_TIMEOUT = 16
    OTHERS = 17
          .
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
ENDIF.
 
 
* Include bdcrecx1_s:
* The call transaction using is called WITH AUTHORITY-CHECK!
* If you have own auth.-checks you can use include bdcrecx1 instead.
" include bdcrecx1_s.
 
" start-of-selection.
 
LOOP AT LT_DATA INTO LS_DATA.
 
" perform open_group.
 
 
perform bdc_dynpro using 'SAPLMGMM' '0060'.
perform bdc_field using 'BDC_CURSOR'
                              'RMMG1-MTART'.
perform bdc_field using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTR'.
perform bdc_field using 'RMMG1-MATNR'
                              LS_DATA-MATNR.
perform bdc_field using 'RMMG1-MBRSH'
                              LS_DATA-MBRSH.
perform bdc_field using 'RMMG1-MTART'
                              LS_DATA-MTART.
perform bdc_dynpro using 'SAPLMGMM' '0070'.
perform bdc_field using 'BDC_CURSOR'
                              'MSICHTAUSW-DYTXT(01)'.
perform bdc_field using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=ENTR'.
perform bdc_field using 'MSICHTAUSW-KZSEL(01)'
                              'X'.
perform bdc_dynpro using 'SAPLMGMM' '4004'.
perform bdc_field using 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '=BU'.
perform bdc_field using 'MAKT-MAKTX'
                              LS_DATA-MAKTX.
perform bdc_field using 'BDC_CURSOR'
                              'MARA-MEINS'.
perform bdc_field using 'MARA-MEINS'
                              LS_DATA-MEINS.
" perform bdc_transaction using 'MM01'.
 
"perform close_group.
 
CALL TRANSACTION 'MM01' USING LT_BDCDATA MODE 'A' UPDATE 'S' messages into lt_messtab.
"perform bdc_transaction using 'MM01'.
refresh : LT_BDCDATA.
 
" perform close_group.
 
ENDLOOP.
 
loop at lt_messtab into ls_messtab.
  CALL FUNCTION 'MESSAGE_TEXT_BUILD'
    EXPORTING
      MSGID = ls_messtab-msgid
      MSGNR = ls_messtab-msgnr
     MSGV1 = ls_messtab-MSGV1
     MSGV2 = ls_messtab-MSGV2
     MSGV3 = ls_messtab-MSGV3
     MSGV4 = ls_messtab-MSGV4
   IMPORTING
     MESSAGE_TEXT_OUTPUT = LV_MESSAGE.
  WRITE 😕 LV_MESSAGE.
            .
  
  endloop.
 
FORM BDC_DYNPRO USING PROGRAM DYNPRO.
  CLEAR LS_BDCDATA.
  LS_BDCDATA-PROGRAM = PROGRAM.
  LS_BDCDATA-DYNPRO = DYNPRO.
  LS_BDCDATA-DYNBEGIN = 'X'.
  APPEND LS_BDCDATA TO LT_BDCDATA.
ENDFORM.
 
 
FORM BDC_FIELD USING FNAM FVAL.
 
    CLEAR LS_BDCDATA.
    LS_BDCDATA-FNAM = FNAM.
    LS_BDCDATA-FVAL = FVAL.
    APPEND LS_BDCDATA TO LT_BDCDATA.
 
ENDFORM.

entry.title: Re: CAP Initializr: Generate SAP CAP Projects in Seconds
entry.content:
Hi there. I'm curious and had some questions, so wanted to try it out, but it's not available at the URL above ("404 Not Found: Requested route ('capinitializer.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com') does not exist."). So I'll ask the questions anyway ... please bear in mind that I'm asking them without seeing it in action 🙂

Doesn't this whole thing contradict the grow as you go philosophy that is at the heart of CAP development?

The decisions up front about advanced features seem also to go against the developer flow, why is that?

Each one of the advantages listed (in each of the sections "Saves Time", "Reduces Errors", "Standardization" and "Extensible Design") are advantages you get already out of the box with the core CDS DK tools. So what am I not understanding here?

I may have missed something fundamental here, so please educate me! Thanks.

DJ

entry.title: Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item Not Displayed in Supplier Line Items App
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have observed an issue in the Supplier Line Items app. The columns Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item are available in the report layout; however, the values are not being displayed. The Purchasing Document Item column is showing "00000", and the Purchasing Document column is blank for the relevant entries.

Could you please confirm:

  • Whether this is the standard system behavior.
  • If any configuration or settings are required to display these values.

Please find the attached screenshot for reference.

Thank you for your support.

Best Regards,
Ankam Chandu.

entry.title: Issue with Automatic Clearing by Ignoring Partial Receipts during Reprocess Bank statements
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue related to automatic clearing in our system and would appreciate your guidance.

Scenario:

We have multiple invoices for a customer, and partial Receipts have already been received and applied to some of these invoices. In certain cases, when a new incoming Receipt is posted, the system automatically clears the invoice because the assignment/reference number matches the invoice and the payment amount exactly matches the invoice amount and ignores the Partial receipts already available.

  • The system clears the invoice with the latest receipt.
  • Previously applied partial receipts remain as open items.
  • This creates confusion in tracking open items and leads to inconsistencies in account balances.
  • The issue becomes more critical in cases where excess Receipts need to be identified and handled correctly, especially for refund processing.

Ideally, in scenarios where partial payments are already applied to an invoice, automatic clearing with a new receipt should not occur without proper validation and should not be automated.

 Thanks

Naveen Kumar

entry.title: Re: Improving SAP HANA DB Performance in S/4HANA: Two Common CDS View Pitfalls and How to Fix Them
entry.content:
Useful hints. Thanks for sharing.

Just q quick technical note:
Wouldn't it make more sense to apply the system-wide configuration changes described in 3513507 - HANA HEX Engine not Used for Queries Containing "WITH PARAMETERS( 'LOCALE' = <code>) " and 2399993 - FAQ: SAP HANA Fast Data Access (FDA), instead of explicitly specifying a DB hint in each and every SQL statement?

entry.title: Re: Why Is My Content Marked as Spam?
entry.content:
Hi,

My blog - https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-cloud-erp-2602-release-for-chemical-industry-2nd-blog/ba-p/14411143 - has been marked as spam and removed. This is 2nd blog in series to first blog released 2 weeks back.

Please check and release the blog.

Best Regards

Manoj Narang

entry.title: Re: Closing Journal Entries Are Missing in Generated e-Le...
entry.content:
Issue Resolved

The root cause was related to the configuration maintained for the year-end closing/opening balance document types. As a solution, I removed the document type assignment for the year-end closing/opening balance entries and maintained the configuration again. After regenerating the e-Ledger file, the missing entries were included correctly in the TXT output.

Another important finding is that the Turkish e-Ledger process is language-dependent in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Since e-Ledger files are generated according to Turkish legal requirements, e-Ledger activities (Run Statutory Reports, Generate Ledger File, etc.) should be executed while logged on to the system in Turkish (TR). After logging on in Turkish and regenerating the file, the opening/closing balance entries appeared correctly in the e-Ledger TXT file.

I hope this information helps others facing a similar issue.

Thank you,

Melissa.

entry.title: Re: Designing Controlled Case Routing in SAP ESM: Restricting Service Team Selection by Case Type
entry.content: Thank you, very informative @adeya !

entry.title: Re: Monitoring Event Mesh from SAP Integration Suite with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: @shivaniborkar Is it possible to monitor the metrics on Event Mesh. Say, if I would like to know how many events have been received on a certain queue. Is there a way in Event Mesh or on CALM to track these metrics? Just like how we can easily see how many messages or API calls are processed on CI and APIM.

entry.title: Solving Problem of buying multiple Assets in one line- of Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I hope you all are doing perfectly fine, its been a while since I posted a blog even the community page is completely transformed and I can head AI from all the directions.

Shifting the gears to AI is necessity, need of the hour, but today I will only solving a problem which is long persisting without AI and using classical SAP solutions. 🙂

So Lets delve into the problem statement - 

We had one business requirement where business wants to procure multiple Assets in single Line item of Purchase Order,  now we all know that SAP has restricted this to 99 quantity only. That will only result into increasing manual work and manual validations, also poses challenges for automation.

Business Example -

Business wants to do the procurement of 500 Laptop, now as per SAP's restriction it needs to be distributed into 6 separate lines of Purchase Orders - First Manual task(increased)

Now while posting the Goods receipt - user need to do the receiving for multiple line items, even though it can be bought in a single line and product is same. - Second Manual task.(increased)

  • This distribution among multiple line-items can be more annoying if you've implemented "Open Text/VIM" 

The reason why it is coming into picture is as for Supplier this is a single Product with quantity of 500, he will share the invoice with a single line-item and quantity as 500 Pieces.

However in client's system this is maintained into several lines, which now again adding a manual work to post the invoice, it could've been automated but this line item mismatch will create an issue, and need the manual intervention.

Proposed Solution - 

 Now as a proposal we wanted to perform the process the way it should be, same example - 

Laptop procurement with 500 pieces, maintain into single line item in Purchase Order

Post the Goods receipt for single line item

perform the automation by using "Open Text/VIM" as line-item and quantity will be exactly the same with supplier's PDF ones.

So what we did - 

  1. Instead of creating all Fixed Assets before the Purchase Order, we start by creating a single manual AUC Asset.
  2. Next, we create a Purchase Order with the Account Assignment category set to "A" and use one Non-Valuated Material that is serialized—this material only updates quantity.
  3. So, what does the line item look like? It shows Account Assignment "A" but uses the serialized non-valuated material with a quantity, say, 500 pieces.
  4. When posting the Goods Receipt (GR), users can post it as a single line item and easily upload serial numbers via an Excel upload option. This means you can purchase as many assets as needed—from 500 up to 999,999!
  5. After the GR is posted, the Fixed Asset Team performs one additional manual step: creating and transferring the amount into the final Fixed Assets, resulting in 500 actual Fixed Assets.
  6. The best part? The invoice process runs smoothly and automatically, with no errors or mismatches.

Thank you for reading this blog, I am preparing something on AI in the space of procurement will share that soon. 🙂

 

Ankur Chauhan

entry.title: Re: stock removal not happening while doing MIGO because...
entry.content: Use movement type 562 in MIGO to post a return of the non-batch stock. Ensure you select the same plant and storage location where the stock currently resides. This will remove the old stock from inventory

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes

entry.title: Re: Improving SAP HANA DB Performance: Two Common CDS View Pitfalls and How to Fix Them
entry.content:
@HarshPatelV 

You are right, but worth adding one nuance: plan cache validation in development is only reliable when done against representative data volumes.
On small datasets, HANA may still select HEX - the engine switch happens under production load, which is exactly when nobody is looking at the plan cache.
That is what makes this pattern particularly difficult to catch proactively.
The broader point stands: execution plan review should be part of the definition of done for CDS development, not a post-incident step.

entry.title: Can the reconciliation / payable account be determined by transaction origin (SD vs FI) for a single
entry.content:
**Title:**
Can the reconciliation / payable account be determined by transaction origin (SD vs FI) for a single Business Partner in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

**Body:**

We are migrating from SAP ECC to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In our current ECC landscape, the same external counterparty is maintained as **two separate master records** — one used through the SD process flow and one posted to directly from FI. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition we plan to consolidate them into a **single Business Partner**.

(For clarity: this Business Partner is set up as a *customer-supplier* — it is both invoiced through SD-driven flows and posted to directly in FI, and we owe a liability to it.)

Our accounting requirement is that the **payable / reconciliation account differs depending on where the posting originates**:

- Liability generated from an **SD-driven** process → **trade accounts payable**
- Liability posted **directly in FI** → **other (non-trade) payable**

We are already aware of the following standard constraints in Public Edition:
- *Alternative reconciliation accounts* (SSCUI 102631) only take effect for manual FI invoice apps where the reconciliation account field is set ready-for-input; they are not applied to automatic SD-driven postings.
- *Special G/L indicators* are pre-defined by SAP and cannot be newly created in Public Edition.

**Questions**
1. For one and the same Business Partner, is it possible to have the reconciliation account determined automatically based on the originating module (SD vs FI)?
2. Given the constraints above, is there any standard approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to achieve origin-based determination at all?
3. If standard cannot cover it, does it require custom development? Under Public Cloud extensibility (BAdI / key-user / developer extensibility), which option — if any — would apply, or is splitting into two Business Partners the only viable path?

System: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (latest active release)
Tags: `S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition`, `FI`, `Business Partner`, `Reconciliation Account`

entry.title: ERS-generated payables from a Purchase Order — can the reconciliation account be influenced (alterna
entry.content:
**Title:**
ERS-generated payables from a Purchase Order — can the reconciliation account be influenced (alternative / non-trade payable) for the same supplier in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

**Body:**

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, when we settle a Purchase Order via **ERS (Evaluated Receipt Settlement)**, the supplier invoice is posted **automatically** from the PO and goods-receipt data, with no manual line-item interaction.

Because there is no manual posting step, there appears to be **no opportunity to choose an alternative reconciliation account** — unlike the manual Supplier Invoice apps (e.g. FB60 / F0859), where the reconciliation account can be made ready-for-input and changed at posting time. As a result, the ERS-generated payable always posts to the reconciliation account taken from the supplier (Business Partner) master.

However, for the **same supplier**, we have business cases requiring **both** postings:
- some liabilities to **trade payable**
- others to **other / non-trade payable**

**Questions**
1. When a payable is created automatically via ERS, is there any standard way to influence or override the reconciliation account, or is the master-data reconciliation account always enforced?
2. For a single supplier needing both trade payable and non-trade payable, what is the recommended standard approach in Public Edition? Is creating a second supplier Business Partner (running ERS only on the trade-payable one) effectively the only viable option?
3. Are there any alternatives we are missing under Public Cloud constraints (e.g. special G/L handling via Automatic Account Determination, or handling the non-trade portion through a separate manual process)?

System: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (latest active release)
Tags: `S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition`, `MM`, `ERS`, `Reconciliation Account`, `FI-MM Integration`

entry.title: Re: BIB Replicating departments from EC to HCM, changing...
entry.content: have you tried this enhancement spot: ES_SFIOM_PROCESSING?

entry.title: Setup Alerts for running WP - Dialog & Background
entry.content:
I would like to set up alerts for running work processes. I have noticed the metric "Workprocess running" is available; however, I would like to differentiate alerts based on the types of work processes, specifically for dialog and background processes separately.

Could you please guide me on how to configure or set metrics/alerts individually for dialog and background work processes?

entry.title: PoC - SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We have a requirement, to present PoC for SAP ALM Cloud.

Presently we are using ChaRM (Solution Manager) for Transporting requests from S4HANA on Premise Systems.

I have provisioned Cloud ALM Tenant in SAP BTP, we need PoC for following scenarios:

  • Test Scenario for Transport Management
  • Monitoring for SAP BTP Tenants
  • SAP Security Dashboard in Cloud ALM

Excited and eager to get this done, where should I start, configuration guides, steps to follow, any guidance is appreciated as this topic is new for me.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition: Automating WHT for...
entry.content:
Good day @dn_eques

For the same intercompany partner pair, standard WHT determination cannot automatically decide at document level whether WHT applies unless the process provides a supported differentiating attribute. If the country version does not support AR condition type to WHT type assignment and the AP inbound posting does not carry usable WHT data, then i suggest you separate the WHT and non WHT flows or raise this as an improvement request.

Best 

Chris

entry.title: Custom field on External Allowance Object
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

A customer of us is facing the following issue:

We have a third-party CICO vendor, which also registers certain allowances. To get the Allowances into SuccessFactor Time Tracking, we need to use the ExternalAllowance object. However, this object doesn't allow us to create custom fields. The customer needs certain data (like location, datetime, ...) of the allowances in the timesheet.

Does anybody has experience with a request like this? Any help is welcome.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Availibility contorl is not working in PR
entry.content:
Have you already read KBA 3505874 - Budget check is not working - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Cost Center Budget Availability Control (Public Cloud) (CO-OM-CCA-AVC-2CL)

  • Check also Scope Item J54

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Elements – Editing Status filter shows English instead of Japanese
entry.content:
Hello,

The "Editing Status" field is generated by Fiori element framework, and it comes fiori element's internal i18 files, so it is maintained by SAP.

Run URL by adding parameter sap-language=JA then it would open.

entry.title: Re: Custom report : Stock In‑Out & Balance Report with Tr...
entry.content:
Hello @Grace_Li1

Thanks for the follow-up.

There is no standard transaction currency MAP calculation in inventory valuation. The proposed periodic weighted average can be created only as a custom management report. Use released stock CDS views for opening quantity, material document CDS views for movement quantities, and PO data for transaction currency values where you can. A calculated measure in Custom Analytical Queries can calculate the result at runtime, but it will not store the calculated value. For persistence, use a reporting layer such as SAP Datasphere or another supported custom reporting approach.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Best Practices for Managing Tax and Financial Data in SAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm interested in learning how organizations manage tax compliance, financial reporting, and accounting processes within SAP environments.

For those working with SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP:

  • What are the best practices for managing tax-related data?
  • How do you ensure accurate financial reporting and compliance?
  • Are there specific SAP modules or integrations that simplify tax calculations and reporting?
  • What common challenges have you encountered when handling VAT, corporation tax, or multi-country tax requirements?

I'm particularly interested in hearing real-world experiences, lessons learned, and recommendations for improving efficiency while maintaining compliance.

 

Spoiler

entry.title: Re: How user can export to XLSX from SALV full screen mode
entry.content:
I like seeing the starting code ("call main") at the very top of the source code, it's why the class definition part is below and so I must use DEFERRED and I must use literal names for members (otherwise, syntax errors). Main is an instance method just because I want to avoid the prefix "class-" for all members. You can't instantiate with NEW before the class definition because the compiler doesn't know yet the constructor parameters -> MUST use create object with dynamic class name. NB: I could remove the DEFERRED line by using DATA app TYPE REF TO OBJECT.

About your "As I said...", I apply the same behavior as you, so I understand very well.

entry.title: Can an SAP Joule Agent be invoked programmatically?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are wondering whether it is possible to invoke an SAP Joule Agent programmatically.

Our use case is fairly straightforward: we have a CAP application with a UI where users can provide input. We would like to pass that input to a Joule Agent and have the agent perform actions in other systems or processes. To achieve this, we would need a way to access or invoke the agent programmatically from Node.js, Python etc.

Is there currently a way to invoke Joule Agents programmatically? If not, are there any plans on SAP's roadmap to provide APIs or other integration mechanisms for communicating with Joule Agents?

Best,

entry.title: Re: Offshore vs. Onshore Instance: China Subsidiary-based Rollout for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editi
entry.content:
Based on Chinese laws and regulations, we believe it is appropriate to proceed with the onshore method.

However, we would like to clarify how the US headquarters can monitor the reports of the Chinese subsidiary under this onshore model. As we understand, the US headquarters and the Chinese subsidiary will operate on separate tenants, which seems to mean there is no direct connectivity between them.

Could you please advise on how the US HQ can gain visibility into or monitor the Chinese subsidiary's data and reports in this setup?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Simulator — Practice SPRO and FI/SD trans...
entry.content:
Hi FranciscoIacono ,
Tcode FCV3, UKM007 does not exist in S4HANA system. FCV3 is relevant to Classic Credit Management and exist only in ERP systems, but not in S/4HANA. UKM007 is a message, not a t-code.
Tcode UKM_DISPUTE also does not exist, additionally anything with 'dispute' belongs to Dispute management.
Regards,
Emöke, SAP

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes
Re: Dangerous goods Custom Attributes
entry.content:
Hello

trying to read between the lines:

Regarding: 3. What is benefit of using additional T.,codes of Substance and specification mgmt over some custom fields in DGP* txns

By the use of the DG process only (using all of the relevant TCODes) you need to maintain DG data by material number

Depending on the number of relevant material numbers: this is not an easy story Any anyhow: you need then to prepare any phrase as needed by yourself

The Best Practise from my point is therefore: you CG02 to prepare the needed data and do DG filling

E.g. a REAL_Sub can have between 1 and noramly not more than 100 materials By using the DG Filng process: any relevant phrase is generated automically and you need to to prepare the data by cobination of material number and DG regulation

C.B

entry.title: Re: Dangerous goods Custom Attributes
entry.content: Topic if custom fields will be handled in different answer

entry.title: Re: Dangerous goods Custom Attributes
entry.content:
Hello

Regarding

1.  Can we enhance the Packaging tabs in DGP* txns with custom fields or we have to use Substance and specification mgmt CG02 ?

Answer is: in theory yes you can extende the DG master data transactions etc. if you acceot do deviate from SAP standard and you have a good benefit in using the new data in Sales, delivery and/or transport management

And you can extend as well the data handling in CGG02 and on top the DG filling process.

Details i need to skip as it s a lengthy topic

C.B.

entry.title: Re: New MSO version 2604 build 199929.20172
entry.content: The root cause stems from Microsoft's central deployment infrastructure, which SAP cannot debug or fix directly. Did your MS web addin store change in the last couple of days?

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Graphical vs SQL Views. Procs and Cons of each?
entry.content:
Hi @silverado23 thanks for this useful article. 

Me, I'm facing the same issue with a Starjoin(SJ) moved out from SAP HANA on prem to SAP DSP by SAP developers as a Graphical View. Because of the original SJ was sourcing a BO universe that cannot be modified, the result in DSP is a huge graphical view: 29 joins, 1000+ Columns and 100+ mln of records.

Current limitation

Admitting that is insane a model like that, our current issue is that SAP DSP is taking minutes to load the view. As also @siegfried_keusch2 reported, the UI is very poor and is performing very bad. 

Considering that the limit to transport packages in SAP DSP is about 25MB (we discovered that by opening an SAP incident due to a transport failure without any error details), there should be a disclaimer or an blocker during the view design that is alerting the user about the size of the view and the limitation in transporting it.

Looking forward to hearing your toughts.

Thanks and regards,

i

entry.title: Re: Dangerous goods Custom Attributes
entry.content:
Hello

regarding this statement:

2.   Class and Characteristics only work with Substance and specification mgmt  and can't be used directly with DG txns DGP*

Not sure what you would lile to get as feedback.

The classes/Charactersics in spec management are used indirect if you perform DG filling

The DG master data shoudl be as "flat" as possible to use the data efficietn in Sales delivery and transport process.

This is the main reason why data hanlding here is different in comparison to CG02 transaction.

But as explained in different answer

1.) you can extend in CG02 the SAP standard at any point in time (if you accept al the risks invilved in that)

2.) and you can use the new generated data points as well dugin DG filling

C.B.

entry.title: Pick replenishment not permitted; storage type (group) not maintained
entry.content:
Hi,

We have set up planned replenishment for two of our storage types in SAP using the customizing activity ‘Activate Replenishment Strategies in Storage type’. However, when we now create replenishment warehouse tasks, the tasks are created to be taken from the same storage type/bin as were they need to be placed. The issue in this lies in the stock removal control indicator, which checks for the storage type where the products are before the stock in our main warehouse. I therefore want to change the replenishment strategy to take stock from the main storage type only. However, when I add this, an error arises: Pick replenishment not permitted; storage type (group) not maintained. In my view, the group and storage type are maintained correctly but i need your expertise in this.

Could you help me resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: Dangerous goods Custom Attributes
entry.content:
Hello

regarding

We have to maintain some attributes e.g, Inner Pack. Qty & Outer Pack. Qty and some more in DG. I couldn't find those fields in DGP* txns,

If i remember correct. You can maintan this data.

PLease check SAP online help, use google and other search optins to find more information

Important: not all fields in DG ,aster data can be "filled" by using DG filling. Here as well you will find hints in AP online help etc.

C.B.

entry.title: Rework order
entry.content:
Hi all,

My scenario given below for ref.

After inspection lot processing - UD 10% goes to Rework location for rectification and stock updated for remaining qty of production order. Rework qty to be processed, corrected and related inspection lot approved and brought to stock.

Now my client wants to know, how to link the rework order qty with parent order.

Kindly pour your ideas.

Thank you in advance 

regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes

entry.title: Re: CG02-Enter char. values for the dangerous goods data...
entry.content:
Hello

in a lot of threads / blogs you will find more help regardign yortu concerns. But as well you will find results in SAP docue as well

Without a good understanding:

What is a LS_UN_SUB, DG_CL_SUB, a Hazard inducer and what is a Dangerous goods regulation: you will not be successfull

The DG data model is explained very well in SAP online help; but the difference of DG regulation IMDG to ADR etc. is not explained.

Wish you the best for your EHS career

C.B.

E.g. check https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/47b7039ad8c44808aafbedb12029db94/b424ba53422bb54ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US and any subsection

entry.title: Blog#2: How Cross Landscape Distribution (XLD) can be integrated into SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
In my first blog, I shared how pony brings custom fields to SAP Cloud ALM. As promised, this one is about the next topic: Cross Landscape Distribution (XLD).

If you need some more details about the general concept of XLD, please take a look at this very good blog of a SAP colleague which describes the functionality in Focused Build. The concept in SAP Cloud ALM has not really changed, so I will focus in this blog more about the technical integration into SAP Cloud ALM.

 

XLD directly inside the Feature 

In standard SAP Cloud ALM, there is no Cross Landscape Distribution on the Feature. You have to leave SAP Cloud ALM, run the distribution somewhere else, come back, and update the Feature status manually. With pony, XLD becomes part of the Feature process itself.

When we open a Feature in SAP Cloud ALM, we find a custom Cross Landscape Distribution area directly on the screen. There, we see:

  • The XLD status: Has the XLD already been executed, is the XLD mandatory  (the so called "Strict" mode) or optional
  • The source system, where the transport originally comes from
  • The target system, where it should be distributed to

So far, this is only visibility. The more interesting part is what happens when someone tries to move the Feature forward without executing the XLD.

 

A consistency check that protects your landscape

We added a consistency check on the Feature status. When a user tries to set the status to "Successfully Tested" (status can be configured) without running the XLD distribution first, an error message (PopUp) appears directly on the screen: "XLD distribution must be completed before setting status to 'Successfully Tested'".

XLD consistency check

 

Distribute the transport without leaving SAP Cloud ALM

To execute the XLD, the user clicks the "Distribute Transport" button in the XLD area. A popup opens inside SAP Cloud ALM with a Guided Procedure:

  • Step 1: All transports linked to the selected Feature are listed
  • Step 2: Add details, for example a description
  • Step 3: Source and target system are shown based on the configuration
  • Step 4: Select the target Features with open transports the XLD should be assigned to
  • Step 5: Summary of the distribution

After a click on "Execute", the distribution gets executed. After refreshing the Feature, we see, that the distribution was executed successfully. Now the status can be changed to "Successfully Tested", because the XLD has actually been done.

XLD Execution

Meet the XLD app

The XLD area inside the Feature is only one side of the story. The other side is the XLD app, available directly from the SAP Cloud ALM Launchpad.

In the app you will find:

  • A dashboard with helpful details on your distributions
  • All connected landscapes with executed distributions, history, and what exactly was distributed
  • Rules, which define what must be transported via XLD. These rules also drive whether "XLD required" is shown on a Feature or not. Rules can be defined per package, object type, namespace, or custom group.
  • An Analytics area that quickly shows the state of your XLD transports
  • Inbound Transports, where you can bring external transports in and integrate them via XLD into your systems
  • An audit trail over all executed XLDs and over everything that happens technically in the background

XLD App

 

A short demo video of the XLD App:

 

How it is configured, without code

All of this is set up with a graphical editor. No developer is required. How the XLD logic behaves, is defined in pony workflows. Our "XLD mandatory" workflow has:

  • A trigger on the status change in the Feature
  • An XLD status check, whether the XLD has been executed
  • A check that blocks the status change and raises an error to the user, when the XLD is still missing

 

In summary: with pony you can run the entire Cross Landscape Distribution process directly inside SAP Cloud ALM. The XLD area in the Feature, the standalone XLD app, and the graphical workflow editor work together to make XLD a real part of the Feature process. No tool switching. No manual transport coordination. No Feature status that does not reflect reality.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Thanks for reading. If you are familiar with XLD in FB and have requirements that were not covered in FB, drop it in the comments. Let's discuss how we can make it possible in SAP Cloud ALM!

In my next post, I will share how mandatory field checks are used and configured with pony for SAP Cloud ALM.

entry.title: Re: HR Trigger functionality is not automatically removin...
entry.content: Since user locking and user group changes are working, the HR Trigger configuration is likely correct. The issue is usually that the Termination Request is not generating or provisioning role-removal actions. Check the termination request details, BRF+ rules, MSMP workflow configuration, provisioning logs, and connector settings to confirm that role-removal line items are being created and sent to the target system. The most common cause is a termination process configured for account locking only rather than full access removal.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Development Tools for VS Code: Everything You Need to Know
entry.content:
Dear @thomasalexander_ritter ,

since it tis officially released, would it be possible to add here 😊 ?

https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#abap

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Improving SAP HANA DB Performance: Two Common CDS View Pitfalls and How to Fix Them
entry.content:
@shais 

Good point, and the post has been updated to reflect exactly this.
For Fix #1, the system-wide parameter hex_enable_with_parameters_locale is actually a prerequisite - not an alternative.
The hint at the view level is only needed on top of it for complex queries or high data volumes.
For Fix #2, FDA is already enabled by default on ABAP kernel >= 7.45, so the hint in the example is per-statement control, not feature activation.
Thanks for pushing on this - it led to a more accurate description of the flow.

entry.title: Re: Behind the Design: How We Transformed the ABAP Development Tools Architecture to Support More ID
entry.content:

Thank You

Hello, 

If you're concerned about or want to keep a track of record and table modifications on the source HANA Database , you can setup audit policies for the likes of DELETES, INERTS, UPDATES etc.


See here  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/b3ee5778bc2e4a089d3299b82ec762a7/ddcb6ed2bb5710148183db80e4aca49b.html

Simon

Philipp

Thank you..!

entry.title: Re: I never received onboarding email Activate Your Account for Identity Authentication Service for
entry.content: "known issue"

entry.title: Re: Supplier Down payment adjustments and tracking
entry.content:
Hi Vagula,

To address your requirement, we need to split it into two parts: tracking the purchasing side and tracking the subsequent sale to the customer.

1. Tracking the Purchasing Side (PO to Supplier Invoice) Yes, there is an app designed specifically for this phase. You can use the Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments app (App ID F2877). This app provides a comprehensive overview of the PO item, the associated down payment requests, the cleared down payments, and the final supplier invoices.

However, to make this work, the end-to-end traceability must not be broken during the bank loan posting. If your finance team processes the advance payment purely in Finance (FI) without a Purchase Order (e.g., creating it manually in the Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests app or posting it directly from a bank statement), the linkage to the logistics process is permanently lost. An FI-only posting records the supplier and the financial amount, but it lacks the material number, plant, and quantity. Without this data, tracking the product later is impossible.

To maintain full traceability, you must strictly follow the PO-based down payment process:

  • Maintain the down payment parameters at the header/item level using the Manage Purchase Orders or Create Purchase Order - Advanced app. Please note: Simply adding the expected down payment to the PO does not automatically create the financial document needed for payment. It merely acts as a commercial agreement.

  • You must actively generate the down payment request using the Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments app.

  • This Down Payment Request is posted with a Special G/L indicator F. It is created as a Noted Item, meaning it is a single-sided entry that does not immediately update the General Ledger balances. It only appears on the supplier balance to be picked up by the payment process.

  • Once the actual payment is made (or cleared), it converts to a regular Special G/L item (e.g., indicator 'A'), updating the G/L.

  • Finally, clear the down payment upon the receipt of the actual supplier invoice.

How does this work with a Bank Loan (Third-Party Payment)? Since the bank pays the supplier directly (and not from your standard house bank account via the Manage Automatic Payments app), the clearing of that Noted Item requires a manual FI step. Your finance team will need to use the Clear Outgoing Payments app (App ID F1367) or Post with Clearing to manually clear the open Down Payment Request against the bank loan liability account. This creates the actual Down Payment document linked to the PO while recognizing the debt to the bank.

You can review the standard process in the SAP Help documentation: Down Payment Processing or here Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments 

2. Tracing the Flow to the End Customer There is no single out-of-the-box app that acts as a standalone report tracing a supplier down payment all the way to a customer invoice. In standard inventory management (Make-to-Stock), Supply and Demand are decoupled, meaning once the goods enter your general stock, the system does not link a specific supplier invoice to a specific customer invoice.

However, full visual traceability is natively available via the "Process Flow" (Document Flow) feature if you use specific account-assigned logistics scenarios:

  • Third-Party Processing (Scope Items BDK / BD3): The Sales Order automatically generates a Purchase Requisition, which becomes the PO.

  • Make-to-Order (Scope Item BJE) or Account Assigned POs: The PO is directly pegged to the Customer Sales Order.

If you use these scenarios, you can open the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app, navigate to the specific Sales Order, and click on the Process Flow tab. The system will visually map the entire chain.

In case you have some specific process in place, please describe it in detail in the comment so I can actually react to your specific business scenario.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Job Profile Builder: Job Profile Approval (Admin Center)
entry.content:
Hi,

This is not working for me. The customer'd like the managers to create and initiate job profile changes. However, we found out that giving Job Profile Draft object permission isn't enough, you should also assign Job Profile editing object permission + Manage Job Profile permissions.

When the manager enters via People Profile o Custom Navigation the workflow is initiated but when entering via Manage Job profile the workflow is skipped.

Any guide or your suggestion is maybe incomplete o contradictory.

Kindly give us any hint ti solve this permission issue.

Thanks!

Homero.

entry.title: Re: stock removal not happening while doing MIGO because...
entry.content: How did they manage to activate batch management with existing stock? SAP standard prevents that.

entry.title: Re: Setup Alerts for running WP - Dialog & Background
entry.content:
Hello,

Please consider in using the "Separate event by label", for example: Wp_type. Then, alert can be calculated separately for different type of work process. i.e:  Dialog, Background, Upgrade and so on.

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patc...
entry.content:
Hi Andreas,

Based on what you've described, the symptoms strongly point to file locking by another process rather than a corrupted upgrade package. The fact that errors decreased between SP05 P4 and SP05 P5 attempts (but didn't disappear) and that the system actually works after the "failed" upgrade reinforces this.

Here are the most likely causes and what I'd suggest checking, roughly in order of probability:

1. Microsoft Defender is still scanning despite exclusions

Adding folders to Defender's exclusion list via the GUI sometimes doesn't apply to all engines (e.g., real-time protection, controlled folder access, ASR rules, cloud-delivered protection, tamper protection). On Windows Server 2022, Defender is particularly aggressive.

What to try:

  • Verify exclusions with PowerShell: Get-MpPreference | Select-Object ExclusionPath, ExclusionProcess, ExclusionExtension
  • Add process-level exclusions, not just paths:
    • setup.exe, BusinessObjectsServer.exe, boe_setup.exe, msiexec.exe, cscript.exe, java.exe, wdeploy*.exe
  • Also exclude:
    • D:\SAP BusinessObjects\ (whole tree)
    • D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\tmp\
    • D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\InstallCache\
    • The temp folder used by the installer (%TEMP%, %LOCALAPPDATA%\Temp, C:\Windows\Temp)
    • The folder containing the upgrade media
  • Temporarily disable real-time protection (Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true) for the duration of the upgrade. This is the most reliable test — if it succeeds, Defender is the culprit.
  • Check if Controlled Folder Access or ASR rules are enabled (Get-MpPreference | fl *Asr*, *Controlled*) and disable them temporarily.

2. Residual CrowdStrike components

Even after uninstalling CrowdStrike Falcon, the kernel-mode sensor driver (CSAgent.sys, csagent) can sometimes remain loaded until reboot, or be only partially uninstalled if uninstall protection was enabled.

What to check:

Get-Service csagent -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
sc.exe query csagent
Get-ChildItem "C:\Windows\System32\drivers\CSAgent.sys" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

If anything is found, ensure a complete removal (you may need the maintenance token from CrowdStrike) and reboot before re-attempting the upgrade.

3. Windows Search / Indexing service

The Windows Search indexer can lock files in subfolders. Exclude D:\SAP BusinessObjects\ from indexing, or stop the Windows Search service during the upgrade:

Stop-Service WSearch

4. VSS / Backup agents

If the VM has a backup agent (Veeam, Commvault, Azure Backup, etc.) or is part of a VMware/Hyper-V scheduled snapshot, VSS writers can briefly lock files. Check if a backup window overlapped your upgrade window. Disable the backup job during the upgrade.

5. The \\?\ long-path prefix in the error

The error Access to file denied: \\?\D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\tmp\... is interesting because the \\?\ prefix means the installer is using long-path APIs. Combined with FileAccessDeniedException from Poco, this is almost always another process holding a handle, not a real ACL issue.

You can confirm with Process Monitor (Sysinternals) during the next attempt:

  • Filter on Path containing InstallData\tmp and Result = ACCESS DENIED or SHARING VIOLATION
  • The "Process Name" column will tell you exactly which process is locking the file (Defender's MsMpEng.exe, an EDR agent, the indexer, a backup agent, etc.)

This is the single most useful diagnostic step I would recommend — it will give you a definitive answer rather than guessing.

6. Other suggestions

  • Disconnect the VM from the network during the upgrade (after taking your snapshot). This prevents Defender cloud-delivered protection and any centrally pushed EDR policy from re-activating mid-install.
  • Reboot immediately before running the upgrade so no stale handles exist.
  • Stop the SIA service manually before launching setup (the installer should do this, but doing it explicitly avoids race conditions).
  • Make sure no Tomcat/Java processes are still running after the SIA stops (Get-Process java, tomcat*).
  • Check Windows Event Viewer (Application + Defender Operational log) at the timestamps in ErrorsAndWarnings.log — Defender often logs the exact file it quarantined or blocked.

About the "successful failure"

What you observed (the system works fine after the upgrade despite the error message) is consistent with the errors being on InstallCache copy operations, which don't always affect the runtime if the actual product binaries were deployed correctly. However, don't trust this — InstallCache is used for future patching/repair, and a corrupted cache will cause the next patch to fail in unpredictable ways. You did the right thing reverting.

Recommended plan

  1. Snapshot the VM.
  2. Reboot.
  3. Verify CrowdStrike is fully gone (services, drivers, files).
  4. Disable Defender real-time protection, ASR, and Controlled Folder Access via PowerShell.
  5. Stop Windows Search and any backup agent.
  6. Disconnect the network.
  7. Start Process Monitor with a filter for ACCESS DENIED.
  8. Run the upgrade.
  9. If it still fails, ProcMon will tell you exactly which process is responsible.

If after all this it still fails with no third-party process visible in ProcMon, then it becomes worth re-engaging SAP support with the ProcMon trace as evidence — at that point the problem is genuinely inside the installer.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Casablanca 2026 | Born in Morocco
entry.content: See you there @Tahemmout

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes
Re: ❓ Question
entry.content:
After you have exited a Payroll Period, you have only 2 options to process data that should have been processed in it before "Exit Payroll".

 1 - Wait until the next Payroll Period is processed, and the retroactive changes will be taken into account;

 2 - Do an Off-Cycle Pay to adress the problem if you need immediate resolution.

entry.title: Re: RAP Draft: Side Effects for Associated Text Fields no...
entry.content: you have to remove it from bo cds

entry.title: Re: Rework order
entry.content: Configure QM follow‑up actions so that rework orders are automatically created and reference the parent production order. Use order type RWR (or equivalent) for rework, and ensure the parent order number is captured in the reference field. This setup allows you to track rework quantities, costs, and inspection results back to the original production order.

entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code is now available on the VS Code marketplace!
entry.content:
Hi!

I'm working on VS Code with macOS, when trying to add a new destination with "ABAP:New Destination" and "connecting to Private Cloud or OnPrem by selecting RFC" I have the error "No system configurations found.".
There is a place to add this configuration?
The documentation doesn't say much about this by the way.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Running e-learning based on large files
entry.content: I have a scorm file and a video, both from 850mb to 950mb. Both are buffering, although far less than 1,8GB. Why is Akamai not handling this?

entry.title: Guiding your customer through the new API policy and ODP-RFC
entry.content:
The way customers connect to SAP systems is changing, and you can feel it in almost every integration discussion.

This session, “Guiding your customer through the new API policy and ODP-RFC”, helps you make sense of that shift and turn it into something practical for your day-to-day work.

SAP is refining how data is accessed to improve security and consistency, especially as AI and automation become part of more solutions. Plus, topics like ODP-RFC scope and non-SAP access are getting clearer boundaries.

Honestly, the thing is, many customers will not notice the policy changes first. They will notice integration behavior, rate limits, or new constraints. That is where your guidance matters.

We will cover governed data access, SAP Business Data Cloud for non-SAP scenarios, and the April 26, 2026 API policy. You will also see how Agent2Agent fits into modern integration patterns and how to plan migrations step by step.

If you are in consulting, sales, or architecture, this session gives you a clear way to support customer conversations with confidence.

Register now and join the discussion. https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014315.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPIntegration #DataGovernance

entry.title: Re: stock removal not happening while doing MIGO because...
entry.content:
This scenario should not occur, the flag cannot be set/unset if

  • Stocks already exist
  • Stocks already exist at plant level
  • Stocks already exist for the sales order
  • Value-only materials
  • SD documents exists
  • Batches already exist
  • WM stocks (quants) already exist

Read this KBA 3225497 - FAQ: How-to change batch management flag when error message M3530 appears

  • Use the described scenarios to restore normal operations.
  • Execute an inventory clearance, potentially reverting to the prior status, followed by status adjustment and stock reposting.

entry.title: Re: AURA Joule HR Agent for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Hi @TimothyPietersen ,

Thank you for your interest in AURA Joule HR Agent.

AURA will be available on the SAP App Store soon. We are also offering a free trial for interested customers to explore its capabilities. Configuration and deployment guidance will be provided as part of the evaluation process.

For more details or to schedule a demo, please reach out to us at kavya.manohara@techenvo.com.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: AURA Joule HR Agent for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Hi @ManjistaAlkem,

Thank you for your interest in AURA.

AURA will be available on the SAP App Store soon. We are currently offering a free trial for interested customers to explore its capabilities. Please reach out to us at kavya.manohara@techenvo.com, and we will be happy to share details on licensing, deployment, and enablement.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: copying dylib into AppBundle and licensing
entry.content:
You cannot include a Developer Edition component in an AppBundle and "put it out".  

The Developer Edition License Agreement addresses this specific question. Here are excerpts

NOTE: ALTHOUGH SQL ANYWHERE DEVELOPER EDITION MAY BE AVAILABLE AT NO CHARGE, THE PROGRAM IS NOT TRANSFERABLE. THEREFORE, YOU AGREE NOT TO DISTRIBUTE, SUBLICENSE, OR OTHERWISE TRANSFER THE PROGRAM TO (OR ALLOW THE USE OF THE PROGRAM BY) ANY THIRD PARTY.

2. Unbundling. As noted above, SAP Sybase SQL Anywhere Developer Edition is a suite of several component software products. Except as otherwise specifically authorized below, use of all components of this Program is limited to the same development Seat(s) (e.g., PC, single user workstation, or real time device) for which you acquired this Program license, and the components of this Program may not be unbundled and used by different or additional Seat(s).

3. Use of Programs.
a. Developer Use of Programs. Each copy of this SQL Anywhere Developer Edition is licensed solely for internal evaluation, development and testing of applications compatible with the Program. ...

c. No Other Rights Granted. Deployment licenses for any other purpose (including, without limitation, usage in a general production environment or on a webserver) must be obtained from Sybase before you deploy or make any other use of SQL Anywhere Developer Edition or any component thereof. You may not transfer or sublicense the Program or any component thereof to any third parties.

entry.title: Offline document upload and download in SSAM 2310
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are using SSAM 2310, integrated open text for documents functionality. We are facing issues with document offline functionality. Below are the steps.

User turn OFF "Cellular data"
Attach document to the Work Order. Perform sync - error - expected behavior
User turn ON "Cellular data" . Perform sync. Document is uploaded against the Work Order. However download failed.

Is this a standard behavior?

 Regards,

Sravanthi

@bill_froelich
